public_reputation: Known around Ithtananalor through day-to-day work as scribe.
appearance: Torva Ironspike usually presents in layered civic fabric cut to show rank without inviting touch, with details that make their scribe status legible before introductions begin.
manner: Listens for contradictions, omissions, and names that do not quite align, then files them where they can matter later. In Ithtananalor, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of scribe in and around Ithtananalor, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Ithtananalor workable through the obligations of scribe while managing the immediate pressure of illicit magic, smuggling, public accountability for harsh enforcement.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of scribe credible while absorbing the strain created by illicit magic, smuggling, public accountability for harsh enforcement.

adventure_hooks.summary: Torva Ironspike can draw the party into a locality problem centered on scribe duties in Ithtananalor.
Torva Ironspike becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with illicit magic, smuggling, public accountability for harsh enforcement around Ithtananalor.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe. V33_1 additive depth pass preserved canon while strengthening at-table identity, appearance, and social motive.
public_signal: In public, Torva Ironspike is immediately readable as a scribe tied to Ithtananalor.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ithtananalor: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Torva Ironspike presents as someone shaped by the duties of a scribe and the daily pressures of Ithtananalor.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a scribe, and proof that strangers will not make Ithtananalor harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Torva Ironspike refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a scribe too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can notice inconsistencies, formalize statements, and preserve proof that others hoped would blur.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Torva Ironspike adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Torva Ironspike always knows more about Ithtananalor than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a scribe in Ithtananalor.
